summary: Iter 43 — Frontend-only verification of v2.7.01 TV-safe-area + single-blue-highlight fixes. Seeded onnowtv-profiles-v1/onnowtv-active-profile-v1=p1/onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:p1, navigated to /, clicked profile-p1, landed on home at 1920x1080. ALL 4 priorities PASS. Hero shrunk to exactly 540px tall (clamp(340,50vh,540)). CW shelf top=600px, CW tile bottom=920.98px — 105px clear of the 1026px TV-safe-area inset and 69px clear of the strict 990px threshold (much better than the brief's measurement of 974.59 because the layout uses paddingTop=32px / paddingBottom=36px on ContinueWatchingShelf and the tile height is 230.85). All 8 visible .vesper-eyebrow labels (Featured · Action, For you, Browse, MOVIES, SERIES, MOVIES, SERIES, UPCOMING · TRAILERS) have computed color rgba(255,255,255,0.55) — confirming the index.css change took effect. CW tile focus ring uses light-cyan rgb(92,223,255), not vesper-blue rgb(0,184,255), so the focused tile's halo is visually distinct from any brand-blue accent on screen — no double-highlight. Section z-index lift (.vesper-shelf-section:has([data-focused='true']) { z-index: 20 }) still works (verified by manually setting data-focused=true → section computed zIndex=20, default 'auto'). Hero ArrowRight regression intact: hero-play-button → hero-info-button in a single keystroke.
critical_code_review_comments: HeroBillboard.jsx L75-77 — height: clamp(340px, 50vh, 540px). At 1920x1080 (HK1 target), 50vh=540 hits the upper clamp; runtime hero bottom = exactly 540.0 ✓. Combined with the ContinueWatchingShelf paddingTop clamp(18px, 2vw, 32px) (2vw=38.4 → clamped to 32px at 1920w), the CW shelf top sits at 600px. This 60px gap between hero and CW is the right size for a Vesper-grade compact layout.

ContinueWatchingShelf.jsx — paddingTop=32px / paddingBottom=36px runtime-measured. Tile height = 230.85px (16:9 aspect at ~410px wide). cwTile bottom = 920.98px → 159px clear of viewport bottom on a 1080p canvas. Even with 5% overscan (1026px safe area), still 105px clear. The user's HK1+projector setup (~972px effective viewport) gets ~50px breathing room — comfortably above the previously-reported clipping line.

index.css L283-294 — .vesper-eyebrow color rgba(255,255,255,0.55) verified at runtime across all 8 visible eyebrow elements (Featured/For you/Browse/MOVIES/SERIES/UPCOMING). The brand-blue→muted-white change is the correct fix for the user's 'two blue highlights' complaint. Eyebrows remain visible (0.55 opacity is well above accessibility threshold on dark bg) but no longer compete with the focus ring for visual attention.

Focus ring uses rgb(92, 223, 255) (light cyan, defined as --vesper-blue-bright or similar) not rgb(0, 184, 255) (--vesper-blue brand). This is intentional — focus highlights ARE distinct from the brand-blue accent. So even if a brand-blue element is on screen (e.g. sidebar active icon, hero year metadata '2019–2026'), there is no ambiguity about what is focused.

Blue-color scan of the rendered home found 5 elements using exact vesper-blue rgb(0,184,255): 2 sidebar nav SVGs+paths (sidebar active item indicator) and 1 SPAN '2019–2026' in the hero metadata. All three are legitimate, non-focus, by-design uses of brand blue — none are eyebrow labels or focus rings. No double-highlight issue exists.

.vesper-shelf-section:has([data-focused='true']) { z-index: 20 } continues to work — manual data-focused=true on the CW tile lifts the section's computed zIndex from 'auto' to '20'. (Note: programmatic .focus() on the CW tile alone does not set data-focused='true' — that attribute is managed by the spatial-nav system on real D-pad navigation. So this test required forcing the attribute. On real keyboard nav, ArrowDown from hero correctly transferred focus down through shelves with data-focused tracking.)
useSpatialFocus.js — hero-play-button → hero-info-button on single ArrowRight verified (no double-fire). v2.6.99 regression remains intact under the new CSS.

Minor non-blocking observation: the welcome-tour overlay appeared in the post-test screenshots even though localStorage 'onnowtv-welcome-tour-done'='1' was seeded. The home page rendered correctly behind it (all geometry measurements came from the actual home DOM). The actual storage key for the tour dismissal may differ from the brief's documented key — worth a future audit but not a regression.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — P0 hero≤540 PASS (540.0), P0 cwTileBottom≤990 PASS (920.98), P0 eyebrow color rgba(255,255,255,0.55) PASS (all 8 instances), P1 section z-index lift on focus PASS (auto→20), P2 hero ArrowRight regression PASS (play→info single-step).

test_credentials: n/a — no auth in app. Seed: localStorage onnowtv-profiles-v1=[{id:p1,name:Tester,avatar:🦁,isKid:false,color:#1e90ff}], onnowtv-active-profile-v1=p1, onnowtv-onboarding-done=1, onnowtv-welcome-tour-done=1, onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:p1=[{id:tt15239678,type:movie,title:'The Bride!',backdrop:tmdb_url,positionMs:1800000,durationMs:6960000,year:2024,updatedAt:now}]. App routes to /profiles on first hit — click [data-testid='profile-p1'] to enter home.
seed_data_creation: Single CW entry 'The Bride!' at ~26% progress (1.8M/6.96M ms). Renders one tile in the Continue Watching shelf with testid 'continue-tt15239678'. CW shelf testid was not exposed (allShelves[].id=null) — shelves are identified by h2/h3 title text.
context_for_next_testing_agent: v2.7.01 is solid for the TV-safe-area + double-blue-highlight fixes. Key facts for future iterations: (1) App ALWAYS shows profile picker at /profiles on initial load — must click [data-testid='profile-p1'] after seeding to reach home (cannot bypass via active-profile localStorage alone). (2) Welcome tour overlay may appear even with onnowtv-welcome-tour-done=1 — investigate actual dismissal key if it interferes. (3) Focus ring color is rgb(92,223,255) light-cyan, NOT --vesper-blue rgb(0,184,255) — these are distinct shades by design. (4) CW shelf and other shelves don't carry data-shelf-id; identify by querying .vesper-shelf-section and matching title text. (5) Programmatic .focus() does NOT set data-focused='true' on the tile — that attribute is set by useSpatialFocus on real keyboard ArrowDown/Arrow events. To test focus-driven CSS rules like the :has() z-index lift, manually set tile.dataset.focused='true'. (6) Hero is exactly 540px tall at 1920x1080 — clamp(340,50vh,540) saturates to 540 at this viewport.
rca of the issue: Pre-fix (v2.7.00 and earlier): (Bug 1) hero clamp(360,56vh,620) → 56vh=604.8 → ~605px on 1080p, plus ContinueWatchingShelf paddingTop clamp(28,3vw,56) → 3vw=57.6 → clamped to 56px. CW shelf top ≈ 605+56 = 661px, tile ≈ 661+90 top-padding+231 tile = ~982px tile-bottom, then 56px paddingBottom → shelf bottom ~1038px, which OVERFLOWS the user's HK1+projector overscan-clipped ~972px viewport area. (Bug 2) .vesper-eyebrow text rendered in --vesper-blue (rgb(0,184,255)) above every shelf. When the user focused a tile (light-cyan 3px ring), TWO blue elements were on screen: the focused tile's ring AND the brand-blue 'FOR YOU' / 'MOVIES' label above. AI-assisted analysis of the user's photo correctly identified this as the second blue source. Fix (v2.7.01): (1) Hero clamp(340,50vh,540) saturates to 540 at 1080p. (2) ContinueWatchingShelf paddingTop clamp(18,2vw,32) saturates to 32. (3) .vesper-eyebrow color changed to rgba(255,255,255,0.55) and CW 'X LEFT' caption to rgba(255,255,255,0.72). Result: hero bottom = 540, CW shelf top = 600, CW tile bottom = 920.98 — 105px below TV-safe 1026 and 50px clear of user's HK1 overscan line ~972. Only one blue highlight on screen at any time (the focused tile's light-cyan ring) — all eyebrows now muted white, all brand-blue uses are static (sidebar nav, hero metadata) and visually unambiguous against a moving focus state.
